title: El Futuro Compuesto & Condicional Compuesto (Perfect Forms)
summary: Habré/habría plus a past participle, reusing haber's irregular habr- stem from Chapters 33-34 and the full irregular-participle list from Chapter 30 rather than re-deriving either: future perfect for an action already completed by a future reference point, conditional perfect for an unrealized past hypothetical, and a third conjecture use (habrá salido ya) that completes the conjecture pattern begun in Chapters 33-34, with an honest cross-reference to Chapter 41 for where the compound conditional's richest use in si-clauses actually lives.
